sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Inter have shown strong form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, with a dominant 4-1 victory over Cremonese and a 4-0 win against Royale Union Saint-Gilloise. Fiorentina, on the other hand, have struggled, managing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a notable 3-0 victory over SK Rapid Wien but a disappointing 2-1 loss to Milan. Inter's attacking prowess and consistency give them a clear edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Inter won 2-1 at home, while Fiorentina pulled off a surprising 3-0 victory at their ground. Historically, Inter have dominated with 4 wins in the last 5 matchups against Fiorentina, suggesting they often have the upper hand in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Inter lead the league in goals scored (19) and big chances created (36), showcasing their attacking dominance, though their defense has conceded 11 goals, ranking 13th for least goals conceded. Fiorentina, languishing in 18th place with just 4 points and 7 goals scored, have struggled offensively and defensively, conceding 12 goals. Inter's superior ball possession (59%) and passing accuracy (86.9%) further highlight their control on the pitch.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Inter's Lautaro Martínez has been exceptional against Fiorentina, averaging an 8.02 Sofascore rating with 5 goals in the last 5 head-to-heads, while Hakan Çalhanoğlu is in fine form with 2 goals recently. Fiorentina lack standout performers in recent matches, which could hinder their ability to challenge Inter’s in-form squad.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Inter's strong home form, superior season stats, and historical edge in this fixture, they are heavily favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 84% probability for a home victory. Fiorentina’s poor league standing and inconsistent recent results make an upset unlikely. Expect Inter to dominate possession and create multiple scoring opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall half-time summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Inter and Fiorentina at the San Siro ended in a goalless draw, with Inter dominating most aspects of the game. Inter controlled possession and created more attacking opportunities, while Fiorentina struggled to make an impact in the final third. Despite Inter's superiority in territory and chances, neither team managed to break the deadlock, leaving everything to play for in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Inter's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Inter showcased their intent from the outset, maintaining a high level of possession and frequently entering the final third. They created a notable big chance but failed to convert, which kept the scoreline level. Their ability to win duels and aerial battles gave them an edge in midfield and defensive stability. Players like Alessandro Bastoni and Federico Dimarco were active in both defense and attack, contributing to Inter's overall control of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Fiorentina's struggles
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina, on the other hand, found it difficult to gain a foothold in the match. Their limited time in the final third and minimal penalty area entries reflected a lack of attacking threat. While their defense held firm under pressure, they rarely troubled Inter's backline. Moise Kean managed a shot on target, but overall, Fiorentina's play lacked the creativity needed to unlock Inter's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: Inter started the game with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes and maintaining pressure for extended periods. Their momentum was particularly strong in several key phases of the first half, where they repeatedly tested Fiorentina's defense. Fiorentina had brief moments of attacking intent, especially around the midway point and towards the end of the half, but these spells were short-lived and lacked the same intensity as Inter's surges.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Inter, Lautaro Martínez remains a significant threat, showing promise in dangerous areas despite not finding the net yet. His positioning and involvement suggest he could be pivotal in the second half. Nicolò Barella and Hakan Çalhanoğlu also stood out with their playmaking abilities, frequently creating openings. For Fiorentina, Moise Kean is the most likely to make an impact if they can muster a counterattack, but they will need more support from midfield to pose a real threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Given Inter's dominance in the first half, they are likely to continue pushing for a breakthrough in the second period. Their ability to create chances and sustain pressure could wear down Fiorentina's defense over time. Fiorentina will need to adopt a more aggressive approach if they hope to turn the game around, possibly capitalizing on any lapses in Inter's concentration. Tactical adjustments, such as introducing fresh legs in midfield or attack, could play a crucial role for both sides.
